description: M8 retrieval golden set. Recall@K acceptance for `memhub eval retrieval`. Matchers run case-insensitive substring checks against the recall hit's title and body, so queries survive row-ID shifts and minor wording tweaks. `kind: match` queries contribute to Recall@K; `kind: empty` queries are safety probes that must return zero results. Two query styles are mixed: keyword-style (3-6 specific terms, ids without a prefix) that play well with FTS5's AND-of-tokens matcher, and `semantic-` prefixed queries written as natural-language sentences that intentionally avoid the durable surface vocabulary — added for re-ranker bake-off (task #20) so headroom exists above the keyword baseline.

queries.notes: Probes the addendum invariant: recall never logs to writes_log.
Stale-embedding UX rule: warning surfaced, agent asks before /reindex.
Load-bearing decision 34 — token-savings rule that makes M8 worthwhile.
Decision 26: contentless FTS5 virtual tables over source tables.
Decision 42: build.rs downloads + SHA-pins BGE-small into OUT_DIR.
Decision 27: eager embed inside the source-write transaction.
Self-referential probe: only passes once M8 decisions are themselves indexed and queryable.
Decision 39: validate_source() at writers, schema stays unconstrained.
Fact 1 in the seeded set — the literal build command.
Fact 2 in the seeded set — the literal test command.
Self-referential probe: the eval harness task itself must be findable via recall.

queries.query: how does memhub know an embedding is out of date
queries.notes: Semantic phrasing for decision 28 (content_hash drift detection). Natural 'out of date' vs durable 'content_hash drift' — minimal keyword overlap, designed to exercise a re-ranker. Matcher uses title only because D28's body says 'Mismatch on read marks the embedding stale' without the word 'drift'.

queries.query: why doesn't memhub trust what an agent claims as truth
queries.notes: Semantic phrasing for decision 17 (MCP tool trust split). Natural 'trust what an agent claims' vs durable 'staged writes for claims'.

queries.query: can I move my project memory from one machine to another
queries.notes: Semantic phrasing for decision 66 (export covers narratives). Natural 'move from one machine' vs durable 'Cross-machine memory transfer'.

queries.query: what does memhub return when nothing matches my question
queries.notes: Semantic phrasing for decision 33 (zero-result behavior). Natural 'nothing matches' vs durable 'Zero-result behavior: empty bundle'. Matcher uses title only because D33's body says 'empty results array' not 'empty bundle'.

queries.query: does reading from memhub leave a trace in the audit log
queries.notes: Semantic phrasing for decision 48 (recall is read-only). Natural 'audit log' vs durable 'writes_log'.

queries.query: what files stay on my laptop and aren't pushed to git
queries.notes: Semantic phrasing for decision 64 (memhub runtime is machine-local). Natural 'stay on my laptop, not pushed to git' vs durable 'machine-local by default'.
Safety probe: gibberish must return an empty bundle, never a hallucinated guess.
